log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a UPX-compressed PE32 executable tagged as 'spreader' and 'runtime-modules', compiled with BobSoft Mini Delphi. It has an overlay and uses runtime module linking. I need to gather more I
The IOC summary shows limited network activity (0 unique IPs/domains in this specific view, though the initial report showed some), 1 dropped file, and 2 processes created. I need to dive deeper into

The network activity tool returned empty results, which is inconsistent with the initial report that showed DNS lookups to corected0.duckdns.org and IP traffic. This might be due to how the tool aggre
The dropped file is 'winup.exe' with SHA256 364d709733a0365f... This matches the initial report. The initial report also showed a second version of this file (2b72282967f763d5...) written to AppData.

The process activity shows the malware uses csc.exe (C# compiler) to execute code and upx.exe to unpack/repack payloads. Notable modules include comctl32.dll, ntmarta.dll, oleaut32.dll, psapi.dll, she

Registry analysis shows no persistence keys, which is interesting given the initial report mentioned registry queries. The malware appears to be a dropper/spreader that uses csc.exe and upx.exe to dep
